summary: The first post-FOMC session faded the hawkish knee-jerk and reversed it in full. NQ reclaimed its ATR trend stop overnight and ground higher all day to close 30,726.5 (+1.94% on the clean September contract), with ES confirming (+0.85%) above its own reclaimed stop. VIX collapsed 11% to 16.40. Both indices flipped back LONG and TSMOM-20 is re-armed. The lone divergence is gold, which did not bounce, falling 1.13% to 4,228 and staying short. Note: today is the quarterly roll/expiry, so index levels are computed on the clean September contracts (the raw continuous print of +3.0% is a roll artifact). Throttle eases from DEFENSIVE toward REDUCED, but size stays capped into Friday's Juneteenth holiday and thin expiry tape.

setups.name: Momentum Breakout / Trend Continuation Long
setups.entry: Long on a hold above the 30,430 rising stop; add on a pullback that holds AVWAP 30,668 / -1s 30,617
setups.bias: Trend repaired and TSMOM-20 re-armed long, but this is into expiry and a holiday weekend. Trade light; demand a confirmed hourly hold, do not chase a vertical grind into the close.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce (pullback long)
setups.entry: First dip to AVWAP 30,668 / -1s 30,617 that holds on a 1hr basis
setups.bias: Buy-the-dip in a fresh uptrend, only on a holding test, never a knife into the band. Losing 30,430 cancels the long thesis.

event.name: First post-FOMC session (relief reversal) + quarterly roll / expiry day
event.consensus: After Wednesday's hawkish-read FOMC, the open question was whether the knee-jerk de-risk would extend or retrace
event.actual: It retraced in full. NQ reclaimed its trend stop overnight and ground higher all session; ES confirmed; VIX collapsed 11% to 16.40
event.surprise: The hawkish interpretation was faded hard and one-directionally, a textbook first-post-FOMC reversal
event.nqReaction: NQ +1.94% to 30,726.5 on the clean September contract. Reclaimed the 30,430 ATR stop overnight (~02:00 UTC), flipped long, and closed above AVWAP 30,668 and +1 sigma. TSMOM-20 re-armed long.
event.crossAsset: Broad risk-on in equities (ES +0.85%, also reclaimed its stop) but gold diverged, falling 1.13% to 4,228 and staying short with no haven bid. Crude whipsawed (72.84 low, 76.06 high) and closed flat 75.54 on a fragile long. Equities up with gold down keeps the firmer-dollar / higher-real-yield read alive in metals.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: No clean tradable gap on the clean September contract; the overnight recovery was a steady grind, not a discrete gap-and-go. No setup.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: The short 1hr leg flipped back long today; watch whether the longer 252-period filter, which held long through the dip, now aligns and confirms the repair rather than diverging.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED (easing from DEFENSIVE as event risk clears and VIX contracts to 16.40)
risk.ddProximity: NORMAL band; TSMOM-20 stayed flat through the event, no drawdown taken
risk.dailyLossBudget: Full (flat into and out of the decision)
risk.sizing: NQ and ES back above their ATR stops restore trend-following long habitat; TSMOM-20 re-armed long, but cap size into the Friday Juneteenth holiday and thin expiry tape. No new gold longs (still short below 4,282). Crude a no-touch after the whipsaw.
risk.note: The FOMC catalyst is realized and faded; volatility is contracting (VIX 16.40, MNQ ATR easing 155 to 119), so the binding constraint shifts to holiday/expiry liquidity risk. Index trend is repaired but the cleanest long location was the overnight reclaim, not the close, so chasing the grind invites a poor entry. Week ahead: Friday June 19 Juneteenth (cash closed, CME equities early close 1:00 PM ET); next full session Monday June 22; no first-tier US release confirmed to open the week, with month-end data the later focus.

psychology: Yesterday the lesson was patience; today it is the discipline not to overcorrect. The market handed back the entire FOMC drop in one session, and the temptation is to declare the all-clear and pile in at the highs. But the cleanest long location was the overnight reclaim, not the close. Chasing a vertical grind into a holiday weekend, when liquidity thins and three-day gap risk opens, is how a good read becomes a bad trade. Honor the re-arm, mark your levels for Monday, and let price come to you.

DirectionUPTREND REPAIR. NQ and ES reclaimed their ATR stops and flipped LONG overnight (MNQ 02:00 UTC, MES 01:00 UTC). Both close above their rising stops (MNQ 30,430, MES 7,522). Gold is the lone laggard, still SHORT below a falling 4,282 stop.High
VolatilityCONTRACTING. VIX -11.06% to 16.40, fully unwinding yesterday's FOMC spike (18.44). MNQ ATR(14) eased 155 to 119. Steady grind, low intraday dispersion (RTH sigma 51).High
SessionNY TREND DAY UP. Overnight reclaimed the stop, RTH opened firm and ground higher all session with no meaningful pullback into the close 30,726.5.High

LiquidityTHINNING into a long weekend: roll-week plus expiry plus Friday Juneteenth holiday (cash closed, CME equities early close 1:00 PM ET).Medium

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 17 to Jun 18)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultGrade
1MNQ reclaims 30,063 flipped ATR stop on an hourly close (repair / TSMOM re-arm)TRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. The reclaim came overnight (~02:00 UTC) and never gave back; NQ closed 30,726.5 (Sep), well above the stop. The call of the day.
2MNQ stays below 30,063 and loses 29,612 RTH low on an hourly closeNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Price reclaimed the stop instead of failing.
3MNQ tags 29,578 (-3 sigma) on a volume-exhaustion barNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Price rallied; the downside band was never approached.
4MES holds 7,400 psych into the NY open (range risk 7,400 to 7,483)TRIGGEREDMIXED. Held 7,400 easily, but broke ABOVE the 7,483 stop rather than ranging; closed 7,574.75. Trend repair, not range.
5MGC reclaims 4,322 flipped stop on an hourly closeNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Gold did not reclaim; it fell further to 4,228 and stayed short.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Monday, June 22, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Friday June 19 is Juneteenth (cash markets closed; CME equities early close 1:00 PM ET). The map is for the next full session, Monday June 22. Filters Monday: Monday so BIR skipped; HOF, MID, BNC eligible (HOF 12:00-14:00 ET dead zone applies). Calendar: no first-tier US release confirmed to open Monday; treat the post-holiday open with normal gap caution.

IFTHENBIAS
MNQ holds above 30,430 rising ATR stop on hourly closesUptrend intact, TSMOM-20 long re-armedStay constructive above the stop; first dip into 30,617 (-1s) / AVWAP 30,668 is the pullback zone, not a short

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Row 1 (MNQ reclaim 30,063) triggered and played out, the call of the day. Row 4 (MES holds 7,400) triggered but mixed: held the psych yet broke above the 7,483 stop to the upside rather than ranging. Rows 2, 3, 5 not triggered (neutral): price reversed up instead of continuing down, and gold never reclaimed.
